source: wynncraft.wiki.gg/wiki/Lootrunning, patched forward with the Version_2.2.0 and Version_2.2.1 changelogs
poolSize: 28 known + 6 unknown = 34 total. The 28 are corroborated by two independent sources (2.2.1 wiki table and the user's guide), which agree on the SET of missions while disagreeing on several numbers — see `conflict` fields.
incomplete: 2.2.1 added SIX new missions whose names/effects are absent from the changelog, the wiki, AND the user's guide (which snapshots a pre-2.2.1 pool). The in-game Inspect > Missions tab is the only remaining source.
note: `reads` = state variables this mission's effect depends on. The union of all `reads` defines the minimum state the tracker MUST maintain. `roles` drive goal predicates. `archetypes` drive conditional follow-up priority.

missions.name: Cleansing Greed
missions.effect: Opening a Flying Chest removes 1 Curse.
missions.notes: Dead without a flying-chest source. Anti-synergy with curse-stacking archetypes that WANT curses.

missions.name: Materialism
missions.effect: All challenges additionally spawn 2 Flying Chests on completion.
missions.notes: 2.1.3 buff: no longer blocks Yellow Beacons, and always spawns 2. Enabler for every chest-consuming mission.

missions.name: Hoarder
missions.effect: Every 6 Flying Chests opened, choose 1 Boon of 2 (at 200% potency) after next challenge.
missions.changedIn: 2.2.1 — required chests 7 -> 6, and the chosen Boon is now 200% potency.
missions.notes: Primary boon generator — lets you skip Blue Beacons. Requires a chest engine (Materialism/Yellow). The 2.2.1 buff (6 chests @ 200%) makes this materially stronger than any pre-June-2026 guide rates it.

missions.name: Jester's Trick
missions.effect: Every 25 items offered from Flying Chests: random +3 Pulls / +1 Boon / +1-2 Curse / +60s.
missions.notes: Multi-role filler. Random, so counts as a WEAK generator — do not let it satisfy the runnable predicate alone.

missions.name: Interest Scheme
missions.effect: Every 2 Pulls gained adds +1 Flying Chest to your next Yellow Beacon (max 12).
missions.notes: Converts pulls into chests; pairs with Hoarder to close a pull -> chest -> boon loop.

missions.name: Orphion's Grace
missions.effect: Boons are 50% more effective.
missions.notes: Universal good. Multiplicative with everything boon-shaped.

missions.name: Opal Offering
missions.effect: On gaining a Curse, consume 1 Boon for +1 Pull, +2 more per 50% Potency above 100% consumed.
missions.notes: REQUIRES per-boon potency tracking to advise correctly. Wants high-potency boons + high curse rate.

missions.name: Gourmand
missions.effect: +2 Beacon Choices per Beacon Reroll used, capped at 6 total choices. Resets when a challenge begins.
missions.notes: At +2/reroll the 6-choice cap is reached in two rerolls, so reroll_spam is genuinely viable. Confirms the archetype.
missions.resolvedBy: user, 2026-07-21 — guide was right (+2), wiki wrong (+1).

missions.name: Route Indigo
missions.effect: Purple and Blue Beacons are obscured but always Greatly Empowered — quantified by the user's guide as equivalent to a Vibrant Aqua boost.
missions.notes: The 'Vibrant Aqua equivalent' quantification makes this scorable rather than vague — but it was measured under the OLD multiplicative model, so re-check under 2.2.1 additive boosting. Obscured = hidden information: the engine knows the tier here even though the player cannot see it.

missions.name: Ostinato
missions.effect: Taking a Boon type you already hold grants +1 Pull per duplicate; boons lose 50% Potency per duplicate type.
missions.notes: INVERTS boon-selection advice: normally avoid duplicates, with Ostinato deliberately stack them. Hard anti-synergy with Opal Offering (which wants high potency).

missions.name: Porphyrophobia
missions.effect: Being offered a Purple Beacon grants +1 Curse. Purple Beacons give double Pulls.
missions.notes: Triggers on OFFER, not on take. Pairs with Equilibrium (curses -> boon potency).

missions.name: Radiant Hunter
missions.effect: +1 Pull per Radiant Challenge mob defeated (max +5/challenge). Cleanse 1 Radiant CHANCE curse per 15 Pulls gained this way.
missions.notes: Radiance curse type is NEW in 2.2.0 — this archetype is under-explored and guides predating April 2026 will not cover it.

missions.name: Equilibrium
missions.effect: Gaining a Curse boosts next Boon's Potency by +100% (max +600%).
missions.changedIn: 2.2.1 — boost +50% -> +100%, cap +300% -> +600%. This is a 2x buff to the whole curse_stack archetype.
missions.notes: SEQUENCING RULE: bank 6 curses BEFORE taking a Blue Beacon (6 x +100% = the +600% cap). The '6 curses' figure survives the rebalance by coincidence — derive it as cap/perCurse, never hardcode it.

missions.name: Inner Peace
missions.effect: Curses are half as effective.
missions.notes: Mitigates the DIFFICULTY of curses without removing them, so curse-count payoffs (Madman, Equilibrium) still fire. That is why it appears in curse-stack lists despite sounding contradictory.

missions.name: Optimism
missions.effect: A rerolled beacon will not be re-offered unless options run out.
missions.notes: Turns rerolls from random into a search. Massively raises P(finding rainbow/orange).

missions.name: High Spirits
missions.effect: +50% Vibrancy Chance.
missions.notes: Universal but low-variance. Changes the offer model, not the decision rule.

missions.name: Beleza Pura
missions.effect: If an Aqua Beacon is offered, all other offered beacons are boosted by that Aqua.
missions.notes: Removes the aqua-then-payoff sequencing cost entirely. Very strong; changes aqua priority from 'take and hold' to 'ignore, take the payoff directly'.

missions.name: Backup Beat
missions.effect: +1 Beacon Reroll per +300s added to your timer.
missions.notes: Reads CUMULATIVE time gained, not the displayed timer. Overcapping is therefore not purely wasteful for Backup Beat — but it does forfeit the +60s/+90s challenge grants, so sitting at cap has a real cost.
missions.overcapRule: Time gained ABOVE the 15-minute soft cap still counts toward Backup Beat and other timer-based mission requirements, even though the timer does not display it. BUT no time registers from entering/completing a challenge while the timer is already above 15 minutes.

missions.name: Requiem
missions.effect: For 15 minutes, mob stat increases are voided — BOTH passive enemy buffs from completing challenges AND any curses obtained.
missions.notes: Time-boxed panic button. Enables an otherwise-lethal curse stack. Track expiry.
missions.resolvedBy: Guide, 2026-07-21 — richer than the wiki, and settles the earlier wiki-vs-guide ambiguity: it voids curses too, not just scaling.

missions.name: Stasis
missions.effect: Timer does not decrease while picking a Beacon (max 5m).
missions.notes: Directly subsidises the deliberation this app requires. Quietly one of the best missions for an advisor user.

missions.name: Chronokinesis
missions.effect: Qualifying chests give +1 Pull but consume 10s (+5s per chest) from the timer. Completing a challenge reduces the penalty by 15s.
missions.notes: CAMP-DEPENDENT VALUE. Far more precise than "non-flying": it depends on CHALLENGE TYPE (spelunk yes, slay/defend/destroy no) and on the camp's environmental chest density. Chronokinesis cannot be scored without knowing the camp, so camp must be a run-setup input.
missions.resolvedBy: user, 2026-07-21 — explicitly non-flying. Wiki right, guide wrong.

counts: Environmental chests (naturally generated in the world)
doesNotCount: Chests generated after Slay, Defend and Destroy challenges

campDependent.best: Corkus — many multi-chest spelunk challenges plus a high density of environmental chests
campDependent.second: Molten Heights — many easily reachable environmental chests

missions.name: Thrill Seeker
missions.effect: +1 Pull per Red Beacon challenge completed, increased by +1 per 7 challenges completed (max +3). RESET by taking a Green Beacon.
missions.changedIn: 2.2.1 — completely reworked from '1 Boon per 5 Red Beacons'. Scaling interval is now 7 challenges, not 6.
missions.notes: CONFLICT RULE: green resets the stack, but green is the timeout guard. Advisor must surface this tradeoff explicitly rather than silently picking.
missions.resolvedBy: user, 2026-07-21 — green reset survives the 2.2.1 rework.

missions.name: Knife Edge
missions.effect: +7 Pulls on challenge completion, reduced by 1 per challenge remaining (min 0).
missions.notes: HARD ANTI-SYNERGY with White/Red beacons and Sacrificial Ritual. Literally zero value above 7 challenges remaining. Classic trap pick.

missions.name: King's Court
missions.effect: +1 Crimson Beacon added to your pool.
missions.cost: Occupies a Mission slot to yield a Trial — i.e. it TRADES a mission for a trial.
missions.notes: The trade is the whole story and the wiki omits it. With only ~3 mission slots, this is not a free '+1 crimson' — it costs a third of your mission capacity. Scorer must charge it the full opportunity cost of the best mission you would otherwise take.

missions.name: High Roller
missions.effect: +1 End Reward Reroll and +10 Pulls.
missions.notes: Stateless, unconditional value. The correct default when no archetype has formed.

missions.name: Redemption
missions.effect: +1 End Reward Sacrifice.
missions.notes: Stateless. Each sac saves 50% of unsaved pulls (2 sacs = 75%, 3 = 87.5%) — diminishing, so cap the stack.

missions.name: Sacrificial Ritual
missions.effect: On finishing a challenge, consume 1 Pull to gain +3 Challenges.
missions.notes: Converts pulls into runway. Fails on the final challenge. Hard anti-synergy with Knife Edge.

missions.name: Complete Chaos
missions.effect: After finishing a Challenge, get an additional random Beacon reward — drawn from beacons still AVAILABLE in your pool, weighted by beacon rarity.
missions.notes: Its EV is computable rather than hand-waved: draw from legalColors() minus {crimson, grey}, weighted by rarity. Because it is rarity-weighted toward common beacons, treat it as a weak generator — it must not satisfy the runnable predicate alone. Note the interaction: taking white/darkGrey REMOVES them from the Complete Chaos pool.

intrinsics.poolLimited: Cannot give effects of beacons already exhausted — e.g. no white or darkGrey effects once taken.
intrinsics.rarityWeighted: Common beacons (yellow/blue/purple) are far likelier than rare ones (orange/rainbow).

missions.name: PLACEHOLDER — 6 unnamed missions added in 2.2.1
missions.notes: 2.2.1 (12 Jun 2026) states 'Added six new Lootrun Missions' without naming them, and the wiki mission table has not been updated. These must be collected in-game. Until then the mission pool is ~29/35 complete, so any probability the simulator reports over mission offers is biased. Surface that caveat in the UI.

renamed.confirmedBy: user 2026-07-21; corroborated by the guide, which labels Sacrificial Ritual 'former Cleansing Ritual'
renamed.CRITICAL: The name carried over but the EFFECT did not. Old Cleansing Ritual was a curse cleanser ('lets you ignore Red and Green beacons entirely'). Sacrificial Ritual consumes 1 Pull for +3 Challenges — a challenge-extension effect with no cleansing whatsoever.
renamed.notes: This is the most dangerous kind of stale data: advice written for the old effect still parses as valid because a mission by a similar name exists. Any guide recommending 'Cleansing Ritual' as a cleanser is describing an effect that is GONE, and its recommendation must NOT be transferred to Sacrificial Ritual. Correctly removed from all curse_cleanser roles and archetype follow-up lists.

sourcePolicy.why: Neither wiki nor guide is uniformly ahead. User confirmed Gourmand +2 (guide right, wiki wrong) AND Chronokinesis non-flying (wiki right, guide wrong). A blanket "trust source X" rule would import errors from whichever was chosen.
sourcePolicy.guideStatus: Re-checked 2026-07-21: the guide Missions tab still lists PRE-2.2.1 values (Hoarder 8 chests/300%, Equilibrium +50%/+300%, Jester +30s, Thrill Seeker per 6). Its Changelogs and Run Setup tabs ARE current. Tabs drifted independently — do not treat the doc as uniformly updated.
sourcePolicy.method: Wiki page as base -> replay every changelog -> override with user confirmation. Each record carries changedIn / resolvedBy so provenance stays auditable.
